expectations.text: Review explicitly notes this is a lightweight review or mentions the change is trivial
expectations.evidence: The review opens with: '**Lightweight review** — single-line comment fix in README.' This explicitly identifies it as a lightweight review and calls out the trivial nature of the change.

expectations.text: Review does NOT produce full Critical/Important/Suggestions sections
expectations.evidence: The review contains no section headers at all — no 'Critical Issues', 'Important Issues', 'Suggestions', 'Strengths', 'Pre-review checks', or 'Next Steps' sections. It is a short paragraph with one supplementary note.

expectations.text: Review is noticeably shorter than a full review — concise and proportional
expectations.evidence: The entire review is 5 lines (approximately 60 words). A full review per the skill template would include multiple headed sections (Strengths, Critical Issues, Important Issues, Suggestions, Next Steps). This output is a single paragraph plus one brief note — clearly concise and proportional to a one-line comment fix.

claims.claim: The fix accurately reflects that capitalize uppercases only the first letter of the string, not each word
claims.evidence: The diff in the transcript shows the comment was changed from 'Hello World' to 'Hello world', matching the behavior of a capitalize function that only uppercases the first letter.

claims.claim: A README.md.patch file is also staged and probably should not be committed
claims.evidence: The git status output confirms README.md.patch is staged. The patch file contains eval scaffolding ('This file represents the before state'), so flagging it as unintended for commit is a reasonable observation.

eval_feedback.overall: No suggestions, evals look solid. The three assertions together form a good discriminating test: they verify the lightweight path was taken (expectation 1), the full framework was skipped (expectation 2), and the output length is proportional (expectation 3). A review that ignored the trivial-change shortcut would fail all three.
